Nexus Labs // Director of QA Engineering

San Francisco, CA (Remote-Friendly) | Full-Time | $280,000 - $420,000 per year

Intelligent Testing Infrastructure for Modern Software

>> ABOUT

Nexus Labs is a rapidly growing SaaS platform specializing in automated testing
orchestration and quality assurance infrastructure. Founded in 2018, we empower

engineering teams across Fortune 500 companies and fast-growing startups to ship reliable
software at scale. Our platform processes millions of test executions monthly and helps

organizations reduce bug escape rates by up to 85%. We're building the future of
continuous quality assurance.

>> SUMMARY

We are seeking an experienced Director of QA Engineering to lead and scale our quality
assurance organization. This strategic leadership role oversees a team of 15-20+ QA

engineers and managers, driving testing innovation, establishing QA standards across the
company, and ensuring product reliability at enterprise scale. You will report directly

to the VP of Engineering and partner with product, platform, and infrastructure
leadership to define QA strategy and drive organizational excellence.

>> RESPONSIBILITIES

- Lead and manage a distributed team of 15-20 QA engineers and 2-3 QA engineering
managers, including hiring, performance management, career development, and retention

- Define and execute the company's QA strategy, establishing testing frameworks,
automation standards, and quality metrics across all product lines

- Design and oversee implementation of CI/CD test pipeline architecture using modern
tools like Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, and Python/JavaScript automation frameworks

- Establish QA best practices, testing governance, and quality gates using industry
standards and company-specific requirements

- Collaborate with VP of Engineering and technical leadership to implement Postman-
based API testing strategies and shift-left testing initiatives

- Mentor and develop QA managers and staff-level engineers, fostering a culture of
technical excellence and continuous improvement

- Drive adoption of advanced testing techniques including performance testing,
security testing, and cross-browser compatibility validation

- Present quarterly QA metrics, quality trends, and risk assessments to executive
leadership to inform product roadmap and release decisions

- Build and maintain relationships with cross-functional partners including DevOps,
Infrastructure, and Product Management to optimize test infrastructure and reduce

time-to-market

>> MUST HAVE

- 10+ years of QA engineering experience with at least 5+ years in leadership or

management roles overseeing QA teams
- Demonstrated expertise with multiple automation frameworks including Playwright,

Cypress, or Selenium
- Strong proficiency in Python or JavaScript for writing scalable test automation and

tooling

 - Deep knowledge of API testing, CI/CD pipeline architecture, and test automation best

practices
- Experience with Postman or similar API testing platforms for building comprehensive

API test suites
- Proven track record building and scaling QA organizations, establishing processes,

and improving quality metrics
- Strong communication and stakeholder management skills; ability to influence at C-

level and across engineering organizations

>> NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with advanced testing frameworks such as Robot Framework or Katalon

Studio
- Knowledge of performance testing tools and load testing methodologies

- Background in infrastructure automation, containerization (Docker), or Kubernetes
orchestration

- Familiarity with security testing, OWASP standards, or compliance-related QA
requirements

- Experience scaling QA teams in high-growth SaaS environments (Series B/C funding
stage or beyond)

>> STACK

Playwright / Cypress / Selenium / Python (pytest, unittest) / JavaScript/Node.js / Postman /

CI/CD Pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) / Docker & Kubernetes / AWS/Cloud Testing

Infrastructure / TestRail/Test Management Platforms

>> BENEFITS

- Competitive salary and equity (0.25-0.5% depending on background)
- Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage with 90% company contribution

- Unlimited PTO policy plus 12 company holidays
- $10,000 annual professional development and conference budget

- Flexible work arrangements (fully remote or hybrid at our SF headquarters)
- Parental leave (16 weeks paid for all parents) and family planning support

- 401(k) matching up to 4% with immediate vesting

>> APPLY

Submit your resume, a cover letter describing your approach to building and scaling QA

organizations, and links to any relevant public work (GitHub, blog posts, or tech talks)
to careers@nexuslabs.io. Include a brief description of your biggest QA team scaling

challenge and how you solved it. Application review begins immediately and positions fill
on a rolling basis.
